evals.prompt: I run a CNA training school in Rochester, NY and want more students to find us online. Help me plan all the directories and listings we should be on.
evals.expected_output: Should check for product-marketing.md first. Should run the Phase 0 readiness assessment (claimable address, Google Business Profile claimable, program page live, canonical NAP locked, privacy/terms, real photos, state-approval status/# and testing vendor). Should treat Google Business Profile as the anchor and NAP consistency as Rule 1. Should recommend the tier priority: Tier 1 core local search (GBP, Bing Places, Apple Business Connect, Yelp, Facebook, Nextdoor), Tier 2 local citations & data aggregators (Data Axle, Localeze, Foursquare, BBB, YP, Manta, local Chamber), Tier 3 school/education directories (Niche, RWM, Trade-Schools.net, AllAlliedHealthSchools, CareerOneStop), Tier 4 state authority (state nurse aide registry approved-program list, testing-vendor locator, ETPL), Tier 5 review platforms. Should explicitly skip SaaS/AI/startup directories. Should reference references/directory-list.md and references/positioning-variations.md. Should set local KPI targets (map-pack rank, reviews, citations).
assertions: Names Google Business Profile as the anchor
Sets local KPI targets (map pack, reviews, citations)

evals.prompt: Can I just copy-paste the same school description into every directory?
evals.expected_output: Should refuse and explain Rule 3: positioning varies by surface. Should note search + AI engines penalize duplicate content. Should explain different framing per surface: Google Business Profile/maps lead with city + state-approved + outcome, citation directories lead with NAP + category clarity, education directories lead with time-to-certify + pass rate, state/authority pages lead with factual approval status only. Should stress that the canonical NAP block stays byte-for-byte identical everywhere even though the description varies. Should reference references/positioning-variations.md.

evals.prompt: Should we pay for one of those services that submits our school to 200 directories for $99?
evals.expected_output: Should say no, citing the 'What NOT to Do' rule against paid mass-submission services. Should explain the listings that matter (Google Business Profile, core citations, education directories, state-approved lists) are free and manual. Should warn that mass-submission services push to low-quality spam directories (DR under 10) that dilute the profile and can trigger spam detection, and that they can't get you onto the surfaces that actually matter for a local school (GBP, state registry, reviews). Should recommend manual submission with consistent NAP and the tracker.
assertions: Notes services can't win the surfaces that matter (GBP, state list, reviews)

evals.prompt: Walk me through setting up our Google Business Profile. We've never done it.
evals.expected_output: Should apply the Google Business Profile Deep Dive. Should cover: claim + verify (postcard/phone/video), use the exact canonical NAP name and NOT keyword-stuff it (suspension risk), pick a precise primary category (Vocational school / Training centre) plus secondary categories, set address/service area/hours/local phone, list each program as a service, add real photos (facility, skills lab, instructors, graduation), write the 'From the business' description, and seed Q&A (cost, length, schedule, financial aid, prerequisites). Should cover ongoing weekly work: post weekly (start dates, info sessions, graduate spotlights), respond to every review, keep hours and start dates current. Should tie completeness to ranking.
assertions: Warns against keyword-stuffing the business name
Says add real photos and services

evals.prompt: How do we get more reviews for the school? We just graduated a class of 15.
evals.expected_output: Should present the graduation review protocol: the week of certification, message every graduate who had a good experience with ONE direct review link (default Google), frame it as helping future students, follow up once after ~5 days, target ~50% response. Should stress you must NOT buy or incentivize Google/Yelp reviews (terms violation, removal/suspension risk), and must NOT solicit Yelp reviews specifically (its filter suppresses solicited ones). Should recommend responding to every review, and spreading across Google first then Facebook/Niche/BBB over time. Should reference the review-request email template in references/positioning-variations.md. Should note reviews are the enrollment driver and a map-pack ranking factor.
assertions: Lays out the graduation review protocol with one direct link
Warns specifically against soliciting Yelp reviews
Prioritizes Google first, then other platforms
Notes reviews drive ranking and enrollment

evals.prompt: We're on Google and Yelp already and got a few listings. What else moves the needle for a local school?
evals.expected_output: Should pivot beyond basic listings to the highest-trust and highest-intent surfaces: getting onto the state nurse aide registry approved-program list and testing-vendor locator (Tier 4 authority — worth more than many generic links), claiming education/school directories (Niche, RWM, AllAlliedHealthSchools, CareerOneStop via state ETPL), building city/program destination pages (/cna-classes-[city]) with FAQ schema, a funding/financial-aid page, and an honest 'best CNA programs in [city]' resource. Should cover GEO for AI answer engines ('best CNA classes in [city]' — get on high-authority local/review/state sources, claim LinkedIn/Crunchbase, FAQ schema). Should cover local press / 'best of' listicle outreach. Should stress NAP-consistent citations over volume and tracking local KPIs (map-pack rank, reviews, AI citations) rather than just submitting more.
assertions: Recommends city/program destination pages with schema
Covers GEO for AI answer engines
Emphasizes NAP consistency and local KPIs over volume
